title: खाटू श्याम चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ दोहा ॥
श्री श्याम चरण सरोज रज, मन में धरि ध्यान।
चालीसा कहूँ भक्ति से, हरो सकल दुख हान॥
खाटू नरेश बर्बरीक, तुम हो कृष्ण अवतार।
करो कृपा दासन पर, करो कष्ट निवार॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय खाटू श्याम बाबा प्यारे।
कलयुग के तुम देव हमारे॥
बर्बरीक नाम जग जाना।
घटोत्कच सुत वीर महाना॥
भीम के पौत्र तुम कहलाये।
मोरवी माता ने पाले बढ़ाये॥
तीन बाण के तुम अधिकारी।
तीनों लोक जीत लो भारी॥
महाभारत में शीश दिया।
कृष्ण ने वर अनुपम दिया॥
कलयुग में पूजे जाओगे।
श्याम नाम से कहलाओगे॥
खाटू ग्राम में मूर्ति मिली।
रूपसिंह ने सेवा की ली॥
मंदिर बना विशाल सुहाना।
सकल जगत में श्याम पुराना॥
फाल्गुन मेला भव्य लगावे।
लाखों भक्त दर्शन पावे॥
हारे का सहारा श्याम तिहारे।
सकल संकट के निवारे॥
श्याम बाबा सबकी सुनते।
भक्तन के दुख सब हरते॥
कंगाल को धनवान बनावे।
रोगी को स्वस्थ करावे॥
विवाह अटके तो श्याम बुलावे।
श्याम कृपा से काज बनावे॥
सन्तान सुख प्रदान करावे।
नौकरी व्यापार सफल करावे॥
न्याय करें श्याम भगवाना।
सत्य का पक्ष सदा पहचाना॥
झूठे से श्याम नहीं राजी।
सच्चे भक्त को देते ताजी॥
श्रद्धा भक्ति से पूजा करिये।
प्रसाद लड्डू श्याम को धरिये॥
ध्वजा चढ़ाओ श्याम दरबारे।
मनोकामना पूरी करे प्यारे॥
एकादशी को पूजा होवे।
श्याम दरबार में भक्त जोवे॥
रंगभरी एकादशी बखानी।
श्याम प्रकट दिवस कल्याणी॥
सालासर बालाजी संगे।
श्याम भक्ति में राजस्थानी रंगे॥
दिल्ली मुम्बई सब में जाना।
श्याम बाबा का अमर पुराणा॥
जो श्याम शरण में आवे।
सो सब विपद विनाश करावे॥
चालीसा श्याम को गावे।
सो सब सुख सम्पदा पावे॥
नित पाठ करे भक्ति भाव से।
सो कष्ट मिटे श्याम प्रभाव से॥
जो संकट में श्याम पुकारे।
श्याम बाबा सब कष्ट निवारे॥
चालीसा पढ़ विनय सुनावे।
श्याम कृपा से मोक्ष पावे॥

॥ दोहा ॥
खाटू श्याम चालीसा पढ़े, भक्ति भाव मन लाय।
हारे का सहारा मिले, श्याम करें सहाय॥
transliteration:
|| Doha ||
Shri Shyam Charan Saroj Raj, Man Mein Dhari Dhyan.
Chalisa Kahun Bhakti Se, Haro Sakal Dukh Haan.
Khatu Naresh Barbarik, Tum Ho Krishna Avtar.
Karo Kripa Dasan Par, Karo Kasht Nivar.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Khatu Shyam Baba Pyare.
Kalyug Ke Tum Dev Hamare.
Barbarik Naam Jag Jana.
Ghatotkach Sut Veer Mahana.
Bhim Ke Pautra Tum Kahlaye.
Morvi Mata Ne Pale Badhaye.
Teen Baan Ke Tum Adhikari.
Teenon Lok Jeet Lo Bhari.
Mahabharat Mein Sheesh Diya.
Krishna Ne Var Anupam Diya.
Kalyug Mein Puje Jaoge.
Shyam Naam Se Kahlaoge.
Khatu Gram Mein Murti Mili.
Roopsingh Ne Seva Ki Li.
Mandir Bana Vishal Suhana.
Sakal Jagat Mein Shyam Purana.
Phalgun Mela Bhavya Lagave.
Lakhon Bhakt Darshan Pave.
Hare Ka Sahara Shyam Tihare.
Sakal Sankat Ke Nivare.
Shyam Baba Sabki Sunte.
Bhaktan Ke Dukh Sab Harte.
Kangal Ko Dhanvan Banave.
Rogi Ko Swasth Karave.
Vivah Atke To Shyam Bulave.
Shyam Kripa Se Kaj Banave.
Santan Sukh Pradan Karave.
Naukri Vyapar Safal Karave.
Nyay Karen Shyam Bhagwana.
Satya Ka Paksh Sada Pahchana.
Jhoothe Se Shyam Nahi Raji.
Sachche Bhakt Ko Dete Taji.
Shraddha Bhakti Se Puja Kariye.
Prasad Laddu Shyam Ko Dhariye.
Dhwaja Chadhao Shyam Darbare.
Manokamna Puri Kare Pyare.
Ekadashi Ko Puja Hove.
Shyam Darbar Mein Bhakt Jove.
Rangbhari Ekadashi Bakhani.
Shyam Prakat Divas Kalyani.
Salasar Balaji Sange.
Shyam Bhakti Mein Rajasthani Range.
Dilli Mumbai Sab Mein Jana.
Shyam Baba Ka Amar Purana.
Jo Shyam Sharan Mein Ave.
So Sab Vipad Vinash Karave.
Chalisa Shyam Ko Gave.
So Sab Sukh Sampada Pave.
Nit Path Kare Bhakti Bhav Se.
So Kasht Mite Shyam Prabhav Se.
Jo Sankat Mein Shyam Pukare.
Shyam Baba Sab Kasht Nivare.
Chalisa Padh Vinay Sunave.
Shyam Kripa Se Moksh Pave.

|| Doha ||
Khatu Shyam Chalisa Padhe, Bhakti Bhav Man Lay.
Hare Ka Sahara Mile, Shyam Karen Sahay.
hindiMeaning:
श्री श्याम के चरण कमलों की रज में ध्यान धरकर भक्ति से चालीसा कहता हूँ। खाटू नरेश बर्बरीक, आप कृष्ण के अवतार हैं, दासों पर कृपा करो और कष्ट निवार करो।

जय खाटू श्याम बाबा! कलयुग के देव! बर्बरीक नाम से जग में जाने जाते हैं, घटोत्कच के पुत्र, वीर महान। भीम के पौत्र, माता मोरवी ने पाला।
benefits: Known as the deity of Kalyug - fulfills wishes of the downtrodden
Provides support to those who have lost all hope - 'Haare Ka Sahara'
Resolves marriage delays, job issues, and financial problems
Invokes justice and supports those who are truthful
Special significance during Phalguna Mela at Khatu Dham
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Krishna, Khatu Shyam Chalisa (खाटू श्याम चालीसा) stands as a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn. This revered text originates from Traditional Chalisa Sangrah. Devotees recite this prayer to grant justice and support those who are truthful and provide support to those who have lost all hope - 'Haare Ka Sahara'. It is also said to special significance during Phalguna Mela at Khatu Dham. It is especially recommended on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Phalguna Mela and Shyam Ekadashi for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 42 verses and takes around 15 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Khatu Shyam Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Khatu Shyam Chalisa in Hindi with English transliteration and meaning. Prayer to Khatu Shyam Baba Barbarik for help in difficult times.
relatedSearches: khatu shyam chalisa lyrics in hindi

seoFaqs.question: Who is Khatu Shyam Ji and what is his connection to the Mahabharata?
seoFaqs.answer: Khatu Shyam Ji is the Kali Yuga form of Barbarika, the grandson of Bhima and son of Ghatotkacha from the Mahabharata. Barbarika possessed three infallible arrows gifted by Lord Shiva, earning him the name 'Teen Baan Dhari.' He had vowed to fight on whichever side was losing, which Krishna realised would lead to mutual annihilation of both armies. Disguised as a Brahmin, Krishna asked Barbarika for his head as a charitable offering before the battle began. Barbarika consented willingly. Pleased with this supreme sacrifice, Krishna blessed him: in the Kali Yuga he would be worshipped as Shyam, bearing Krishna's own name and form, and devotees who approached him with sincere hearts would have their wishes fulfilled. (Sources: Vedaz; The Civil India)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Khatu Shyam called 'Hare Ka Sahara'?
seoFaqs.answer: 'Hare Ka Sahara' literally means 'the support of the defeated' or 'the refuge of the losers.' This title is rooted in Barbarika's original vow to always fight for the losing side — a vow Krishna later transmuted into a cosmic blessing. As Khatu Shyam, Barbarika is believed to come to the aid of devotees who feel beaten down by life: those struggling with disease, debt, family disputes, or personal failures. The popular devotional saying — 'Hare ka sahara, Baba Shyam hamara' — captures this faith. It is one reason Khatu Shyam Ji's devotee base has grown rapidly across Rajasthan, Haryana, Delhi, and Gujarat, particularly among traders and people facing prolonged hardship. (Source: Real She Power)

seoFaqs.question: How was the Khatu Shyam temple in Rajasthan discovered?
seoFaqs.answer: According to local tradition, Barbarika's severed head was buried at the village of Khatu in Sikar district, Rajasthan, and was lost to memory for centuries. The site was rediscovered when villagers noticed a cow spontaneously releasing milk over a particular spot in the earth. When they dug, they unearthed the sacred head. The local king, Roop Singh Chauhan, then had a dream in which he was instructed to build a temple and install the holy relic. Following the divine command, the original Khatu Shyam Mandir was constructed in 1027 CE. The temple was significantly renovated in the late 17th century by Diwan Abhai Singh, who rebuilt the inner sanctum and installed the present murti. (Source: Vedaz; Cottage9)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Phalgun Mela at Khatu Shyam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Phalgun Mela is the largest annual festival at the Khatu Shyam Mandir, held during the Hindu month of Phalgun (February-March) between the Ashtami and Dwadashi tithis of the Shukla Paksha. The fair commemorates the day Barbarika's head is believed to have manifested at Khatu. Lakhs of devotees travel from across North India, many on foot from Reengus, Sikar, and Jaipur, carrying the Shyam dhwaja (flag) as an offering. Bhajan sandhyas, nishan ceremonies, and night-long jagrans are central to the mela. The Phalgun Mela typically draws between 10 and 30 lakh pilgrims, making it one of Rajasthan's largest religious gatherings. Devotees believe darshan during this period grants the highest punya. (Source: Cottage9; Temple Story)

seoFaqs.question: What is the structure and origin of the Khatu Shyam Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: The Khatu Shyam Chalisa follows the standard chalisa form: forty chaupais bracketed by an opening and closing doha, totalling 43 verses, modelled on Tulsidas's Hanuman Chalisa template. Unlike the Hanuman Chalisa, however, no single canonical author is universally attributed; the text emerged from the Shyam bhakti tradition that flourished in Rajasthan and Delhi from the 19th century onwards. The verses narrate Barbarika's birth, his three arrows, his vow, the encounter with Krishna, the granting of the Kali Yuga boon, the discovery of the head at Khatu village, and the establishment of the temple. Each chaupai ends with phrases like 'Jai Shri Shyam' or 'Hare ka sahara,' making the text easy to chant in groups during Shyam jagrans and bhajan sandhyas. (Sources: Khatu Chalisa; Bhakti Nidhi)
